document.docAuthor: Peng, Feng, Xie, Xiufeng, Peng, Zhengqiang, Wang, Xingmin & Chen, Xiaoshen
document.masterDocTitle: A taxonomic review of the genus Axinoscymnus Kamiya, with descriptions of three new species (Coleoptera, Coccinellidae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: A taxonomic review of the genus Axinoscymnus Kamiya, with descriptions of three new species (Coleoptera, Coccinellidae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Key Laboratory of Bio-Pesticide Innovation and Application, Guangdong Province, Guangzhou 510642, China. & Engineering Research Center of Biocontrol, Ministry of Education and Guangdong Province, Guangzhou 510640, China.

bibRefCitation.author: Kamiya, H.
bibRefCitation.refString: Kamiya, H. (1963) On a new genus and species of Coccinellidae (Coleoptera) feeding on whiteflies. Mushi, 37, 127 - 130.

paragraph.treatmentCitationGroup:
Axinoscymnus beneficus Kamiya, 1963: 128
.—
Kamiya 1965: 99
,
Sasaji 1971: 81
,
Yang et Wu 1972: 125
,
Ren et Pang 1992: 20
,
Yu 1995: 128
,
Yu et Lau 2001: 153
,
Pang et al . 2004: 90
,
Ren et al . 2009: 54
.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Diagnosis. This species is similar to
A . singaporicus Pang et Gordon
in the elytra with brown basal margin but can be separated from the latter by the pointed apex of penis ( Fig. 7e ). In
A. singaporicus
, the apex of penis is distinctly swollen and blunt according to the illustrations given by Pang & Gordon (1986) .
Female genitalia. Coxites elongate ( Fig. 1j ), subtriangular, without styli. Spermatheca short and stout, bulbousshaped as shown in Fig. 1i .

Material examined.

30. IX. 2004
, collector unknown.
